subject: hid-microsoft: support for XBox Elite 2 paddles.
author: Sorgelig

impact.effect_if_absent: Xbox Elite 2 controller Bluetooth paddle buttons (BTN_GRIPL/L2/R/R2) become unavailable to userspace; generic HID reports them as unmapped consumer usages and trigger-scale switch

userspace_coupling.interface: Linux input subsystem EV_KEY button events: BTN_GRIPL (0x224), BTN_GRIPL2 (0x225), BTN_GRIPR (0x226), BTN_GRIPR2 (0x227)
userspace_coupling.main_mister_ref: Main_MiSTer/MiSTer.ini:2177 (xbe2_shift option mentioning 0x224); Main_MiSTer/joymapping.cpp:map_paddle_btn(); Main_MiSTer/input.cpp:paddle mapping infrastructure

forward_port.effort: minimal — device-table macro renamed (USB_DEVICE_ID_MS_XBOX_ELITE2_CONTROLLER→USB_DEVICE_ID_MS_XBOX_CONTROLLER_MODEL_1797_BLE, same numeric ID 0x0b22); BTN_GRIPL/R/L2/R2 unconditionally available in 6.18 input-event-codes.h so #ifndef guards removed

notes: This commit adds MS_QUIRK_ELITE2_PADDLES handling to hid-microsoft driver for Xbox Elite 2 over Bluetooth (device ID 0x0b22, branded USB_DEVICE_ID_MS_XBOX_CONTROLLER_MODEL_1797_BLE in 6.18). The fork's original commit defined BTN_GRIPL/R/L2/R2 with #ifndef guards (now dead code in 6.18 where these are unconditional in input-event-codes.h). Paddle decoding: MS_XBE2_PADDLE_USAGE (HID_UP_CONSUMER | 0x0081) bitmask value → BIT(0)=BTN_GRIPR, BIT(1)=BTN_GRIPR2, BIT(2)=BTN_GRIPL, BIT(3)=BTN_GRIPL2. Trigger-scale usage (0x0099) is ignored entirely. Vanilla 6.18.38 lists the Elite 2 device but with only MS_QUIRK_FF (force feedback), no paddle handling — hence equivalence='contradicted' (patch adds functionality absent upstream). Carried patch verified to match fork commit (0016-hid-microsoft-elite2-paddles.patch line 1: 'From c784a6856d73d78cfd37e4bd0011b186523e31c5'). Forward-port is clean with device-table macro rename only. Failure mode is silent (paddles simply become unmapped HID usages if patch is dropped). Userspace coupling confirmed via Main_MiSTer configuration and paddle-axis handling code, though button codes are generic and available through standard input event mapping.
